events.message: Triage started for DEN-007 (UnitedHealthcare, $225.00 at stake, 1 past lesson(s) injected).

lessons.lesson: UHC 16/N54 on an E/M billed with a same-day procedure is usually a missing modifier 25, not missing documentation — run ncci_edit_check before attaching records.

events.message: I'll follow the lesson and start with the CARC lookup and NCCI edit check simultaneously before drawing any conclusions.

result.description: Claim/service lacks information or has submission/billing error(s).
result.typical_root_cause: Missing or invalid data element (member ID, dx pointer, NDC, attachment)
result.typical_fix: Read the RARC to find the missing element; supply it and resubmit. Beware: payers sometimes use 16 for edits that are really bundling or modifier problems

citations.quote: Claim/service lacks information or has submission/billing error(s).
Claim information is inconsistent with the billed services or provider.

result.rarc_meaning: Claim information is inconsistent with the billed services or provider.

edits.rationale: E/M on the same day as knee arthroscopy bundles unless a separately identifiable service is documented; append modifier 25 to the E/M

citation.quote: E/M on the same day as knee arthroscopy bundles unless a separately identifiable service is documented; append modifier 25 to the E/M

events.message: The NCCI edit confirms it: 99213 bundles into 29881 with modifier_indicator = 1 (bypassable). The lesson is validated — this is a missing modifier 25, not missing documentation. Let me check resubmission history before proposing the fix.

matches.summary: UnitedHealthcare denial CARC 97/M80. Fix applied: Appended modifier 25 to the E/M billed with a same-day 20610 injection. Outcome: paid after 14 days. Notes: Separately identifiable E/M documented; per UHC-CP-044.

UnitedHealthcare denial CARC 16/N54. Fix applied: Appended modifier 25 to the E/M billed same day as an arthroscopy. Outcome: paid after 15 days. Notes: Second attempt on the same claim — real cause was bundling, not missing info.

UnitedHealthcare denial CARC 16/N54. Fix applied: Resubmitted unchanged with medical records attached. Outcome: denied_again after 21 days. Notes: 16/N54 was masking an NCCI bundling edit — attachments did not address it.

UnitedHealthcare denial CARC 18/N522. Fix applied: Resubmitted the same claim a third time. Outcome: denied_again after 9 days. Notes: Original had already paid — resubmitting duplicates only creates rework.

events.message: RSB-006 is a near-perfect match — exact same payer, CARC 16/N54, same-day arthroscopy, modifier 25 fix → **paid in 15 days**. RSB-005 also confirms that attaching records *without* the modifier fix fails. I have everything I need.

Root cause: bundling_ncci — The 99213 E/M was denied because it bundles with the same-day 29881 arthroscopy under NCCI edits. UHC masked this as CARC 16 "missing information," but the true cause is the absent modifier 25, which is required to signal a separately identifiable E/M service (left-shoulder evaluation, M25.511) distinct from the knee procedure.

events.message: Fix validated. Now commit the decision.

payload.rationale: Root cause is a bypassable NCCI bundling edit (modifier_indicator=1) between 99213 and 29881. Adding modifier 25 to line 1 is the validated, minimal, field-whitelisted correction. Precedent RSB-006 (identical payer/CARC/fix) paid in 15 days. Value at stake is $225.00 (below the $1,000 guardrail). Confidence is 0.95 (above 0.85 guardrail). Fix proposal was validated (FIX-DEN-007-011). All three auto_fix_resubmit guardrail conditions are met.

payload.guardrail_note: Auto path allowed: confidence 0.95 > 0.85 and value $225.00 < $1000.00.

rejected_routes.appeal: No clinical dispute or coverage disagreement exists — this is a pure coding correction (missing modifier 25); appeal is unnecessary overhead.
rejected_routes.write_off: The denial is fully correctable with a modifier addition; writing off $225 would be an unrecovered loss with no justification.
rejected_routes.human_review: Root cause is unambiguous (NCCI edit, confirmed precedent, validated fix); no human judgment is needed to resolve this case.
